name: Roasted Figs in Honey-Lime Syrup
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F.
2. Remove stems from the figs and cut each fig lengthwise into quarters without cutting through the bottom.
3. Place the figs in a 9-inch pie plate or shallow baking dish.
4. Using a fine metal grater or citrus zester, remove the green peel from the lime and set aside.
5. Cut the lime in half and squeeze to extract the juice.
6. In a 1-quart saucepan over low heat or microwave-safe cup in the microwave oven, melt honey and butter together until smooth; then remove from heat and stir in the lime juice.
7. Drizzle the honey-lime syrup over the figs.
8. Bake the figs for 15 minutes, basting once with the syrup from the dish.
9. Sprinkle grated lime rind over the figs.
10. On each serving plate, arrange 3 figs and some honey-lime syrup.
11. Serve with a dollop of creme fraiche and garnish with a lime wedge if desired.
